content: MVRV RATIO — Cycle Top/Bottom Detection: MVRV = Market Value / Realized Value. Market Value = current market cap (spot price × supply). Realized Value = each UTXO valued at the price it last moved on-chain. MVRV > 3.5 = euphoric unrealized profits, historically signals cycle TOP. MVRV < 1.0 = realized value exceeds market value = deep capitulation, asymmetric accumulation opportunity. MVRV between 1.0-2.0 = neutral/undervalued zone. MVRV 2.0-3.0 = warming up. The ratio quantifies aggregate unrealized profit/loss of the entire network — it maps the psychological state of all holders simultaneously.

NUPL (Net Unrealized Profit/Loss) — Supply Distribution Tracking: NUPL = (Market Cap - Realized Cap) / Market Cap. Maps proportion of circulating supply in profit vs loss. A UTXO is 'in profit' if current price > acquisition price. Track NUPL VELOCITY (rate of change) for advanced warning: rapid NUPL increase during markup = LTH distributing to new buyers. Rapid NUPL decrease during markdown = smart money accumulating from capitulating sellers. NUPL zones: >0.75 = Euphoria (sell signal), 0.5-0.75 = Belief/Greed, 0.25-0.5 = Hope/Optimism, 0-0.25 = Anxiety/Fear, <0 = Capitulation (strong buy signal).

SOPR (Spent Output Profit Ratio) — Realized Profit/Loss on Active Spends: SOPR = value of outputs at spend time / value of outputs at creation time. SOPR > 1.0 = coins being spent at profit (market realizing gains). SOPR < 1.0 = coins being spent at loss (capitulation). KEY SIGNAL: SOPR reset to 1.0 after capitulation (SOPR < 1.0) = high-probability re-entry. When SOPR resets from below 1.0 back to 1.0, it means loss-selling exhaustion is complete. This is one of the most reliable on-chain buy signals. During euphoria, SOPR consistently > 1.0 with diminishing magnitude = distribution phase.

NVT RATIO AND NVT SIGNAL — Network Valuation vs Utility: NVT Ratio = Market Cap / Daily On-Chain Transaction Volume. Functions like a P/E ratio for blockchain. High NVT = market cap growing faster than transaction volume = overvalued/speculation-driven. Low NVT = undervalued/settlement-driven. NVT SIGNAL replaces raw daily volume with 90-day MA to smooth volatility. Descending NVT Signal = on-chain volume outpacing market cap = fundamentally undervalued. Ascending NVT Signal = speculation outpacing utility = overvalued. Use NVT Signal, not raw NVT, for trading decisions.

EXCHANGE INFLOW/OUTFLOW — Leading Indicator: Exchange inflow = BTC moving TO exchanges = bearish (preparing to sell). Exchange outflow = BTC moving FROM exchanges = bullish (moving to cold storage/holding). NET FLOW is the key metric: sustained negative net flow (outflow > inflow) = accumulation phase, supply squeeze building. Sudden large inflow spike = potential sell pressure incoming. Exchange balance at multi-year lows = supply squeeze, bullish for price. Track 7-day and 30-day moving averages of flows — daily values are too noisy. Stablecoin inflow to exchanges = dry powder waiting to buy.

STABLECOIN MINTING FLOWS — Buy Signal: When Tether (USDT) or USDC mints large batches of new stablecoins, it signals institutional capital preparing to enter the market. Track total stablecoin supply as a leading indicator. Rapid stablecoin supply increase during market fear = smart money deploying dry powder. Stablecoin supply decrease = capital leaving crypto. USDT dominance (USDT market cap / total crypto market cap) rising during fear = capital flight to safety WITHIN crypto (not leaving). USDT dominance falling during rally = capital rotating into risk assets.

LONG-TERM HOLDER ACCUMULATION PATTERNS: LTH = wallets holding >155 days. During bear markets, LTH accumulate aggressively from panic sellers. LTH supply increasing while price falls = smart money accumulation (bullish divergence). LTH supply decreasing while price rises = distribution phase (bearish divergence). The transition from LTH accumulation to distribution marks the shift from bear to bull market. When LTH holdings reach cycle highs and start declining = distribution has begun. When LTH holdings reach cycle lows and start increasing = accumulation has begun. This is the most reliable cycle indicator.

ON-CHAIN AI AGENTS — Automated Signal Extraction: Analytics platforms are developing AI-driven trading agents that consume fragmented on-chain intelligence across DEXs, stablecoin minting flows, and money markets to execute trades autonomously. These agents strip human cognitive bias from execution. Key capabilities: wallet clustering (identify whale wallets), smart money tracking (follow profitable addresses), DEX flow analysis (detect large swaps before they move price), cross-chain capital flow tracking. The edge is in SPEED of data ingestion, not the data itself — everyone has access to the same blockchain.

REALIZED CAP VS MARKET CAP DIVERGENCE: Realized Cap values each coin at its last on-chain movement price — represents the aggregate cost basis of all holders. When Market Cap >> Realized Cap = massive unrealized profits, euphoria. When Market Cap approaches Realized Cap = near cost basis, high risk of capitulation. When Market Cap < Realized Cap = aggregate loss, deep value zone. The GAP between Market Cap and Realized Cap is the most reliable measure of market overheating or undervaluation. Track the ratio (MVRV) rather than absolute values.

MVRV Z-SCORE — Quantitative Thresholds: Z-Score = (Market Value - Realized Value) / StdDev(Market Value). Historical thresholds: Z > 7 = extreme euphoria, cycle top imminent (sell). Z > 5 = very overheated. Z 2-5 = bull market territory. Z 0-2 = neutral. Z < 0 = undervalued, accumulation zone. Z < -0.5 = deep capitulation, strongest buy signal. The Z-Score normalizes MVRV across different market cap eras, making it comparable across cycles. Bitcoin has NEVER sustained a bull market with Z-Score declining — divergence between price and Z-Score is a warning.

SOPR RESET AS RE-ENTRY SIGNAL: The SOPR reset is one of the highest-conviction on-chain buy signals. Mechanism: During capitulation, SOPR < 1.0 (coins moving at loss). As loss-selling exhaustion completes, SOPR resets back to 1.0. This reset = the marginal seller has been absorbed. Entry: Buy when SOPR crosses back above 1.0 after sustained period below 1.0. Confirmation: Combine with exchange outflow spike (smart money absorbing sell pressure). Historical accuracy: Every major Bitcoin bottom since 2011 has been preceded by a SOPR reset. False signals are rare but occur during prolonged bear markets.

EXCHANGE BALANCE MULTI-YEAR LOWS: Total BTC held on exchanges declining to multi-year lows = supply squeeze. When exchange balance is low, any demand increase creates outsized price moves because there's less supply available for sale. Track: 1) Total exchange balance (declining = bullish), 2) Exchange balance rate of change (accelerating decline = very bullish), 3) Exchange balance relative to total supply. Current trend: Exchange balance has been in structural decline since 2020 as BTC moves to cold storage and ETF custody. This is a long-term bullish structural shift.

WALLET TRACKING VIA ON-CHAIN DATA: Track wallets with >1000 BTC for accumulation/distribution signals. Whale accumulation pattern: Multiple large outflows from exchange to cold storage within short timeframe. Whale distribution pattern: Large transfers from cold storage to exchange. Tools: Whale Alert (real-time large transactions), Blockchain.com (wallet clustering), Glassnode (cohort analysis). Key insight: Whales accumulate during fear and distribute during euphoria — track their behavior as a contrarian signal. A single whale transfer is noise; a PATTERN of transfers is signal.

NVT DIVERGENCE — Price vs Network Activity: When price makes higher highs but NVT Signal is ALSO making higher highs = bearish divergence (price rising on speculation, not utility). When price makes lower lows but NVT Signal is declining = bullish divergence (network usage holding up despite price decline). NVT divergence is most useful during ranging markets to determine which direction the next breakout will go. Combine with volume profile: if NVT shows undervaluation AND price is at a high-volume node = strong support.

UTXO AGE DISTRIBUTION FOR CYCLE POSITIONING: UTXO age bands show what percentage of supply last moved in various timeframes (1d, 1w, 1m, 3m, 6m, 1y, 2y, 5y+). Young UTXOs (1d-1w) dominating = high speculation, active trading. Old UTXOs (1y+) declining = long-term holders selling (distribution). Old UTXOs (1y+) increasing = long-term holders accumulating (accumulation). The shift from old UTXO dominance to young UTXO dominance marks the transition from accumulation to markup. When 1y+ UTXOs reach 60%+ of supply = deep accumulation, cycle bottom likely near.
